project.name: GuardTrack E-Learning Platform
project.description:
## Goal
Build a fully navigable frontend for an internal e-learning platform for security guards deployed at Dutch events and festivals. 4 pages (Dashboard, Catalogus, Cursus Detail, Profiel), responsive design, Dutch UI, rich mock data, interactive quizzes, and a shield-themed design system.

## Technical Spec Reference
`.planning/guardtrack-elearning/technical-spec.md`

## Architecture Context
- **Pattern:** Next.js 15 App Router, feature-based pages, shared component library
- **Key directories:** `src/app/`, `src/components/`, `src/data/mock/`, `src/lib/`
- **Test strategy:** Visual validation at 375px/768px/1280px breakpoints, interactive testing (quizzes, navigation, search/filter)

## Milestones

### 1. Foundation
Project scaffolding, design system tokens, TypeScript types, mock data, and responsive layout shell.

### 2. Component Library
All shared UI components: shield progress, course cards, badges, stats, certifications, deployments.

### 3. Pages
All 4 application pages: Dashboard, Course Catalog, Course Detail with lessons/quizzes, User Profile with radar chart.

## Required MCP Tools
- `mcp__plugin_sero-workflow_linear__*` — issue tracking
- `mcp__plugin_sero-workflow_shadcn__*` — shadcn/ui component installation

## Out of Scope
- Backend API / database / authentication
- Real video playback (placeholder only)
- Push notifications, admin views
- Multi-language (i18n), dark mode
- E2E / unit tests
- Deployment to production

## Completion Criteria
- [ ] All issues resolved and PRs merged
- [ ] All 4 pages render correctly at mobile (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1280px)
- [ ] Interactive quizzes work in both practice and test mode
- [ ] Navigation between all pages works with active state indicators
- [ ] All UI text is in Dutch
- [ ] `npm run dev` starts without errors and `npm run build` succeeds

milestones.name: Phase 1: Foundation
milestones.description: Project scaffolding, design system tokens, TypeScript types, mock data layer, and responsive layout shell with navigation.

milestones.name: Phase 2: Component Library
milestones.description: All shared UI components used across pages: shield progress indicator, course cards, badges, stats cards, certification cards, deployment cards, and leaderboard rows.

milestones.name: Phase 3: Pages
milestones.description: All 4 application pages: Dashboard (briefing view), Course Catalog (search/filter), Course Detail (lessons/quizzes/scenarios), and User Profile (certs/radar/history).

issues.title: [Foundation] Project Scaffolding & Design System Setup
issues.description:
## Context

This is the first issue for the GuardTrack E-Learning Platform — a frontend-only Next.js 15 application for security guard training. Everything else depends on this scaffolding being correct.

## What

Initialize a Next.js 15 project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, and shadcn/ui. Configure the design system tokens from `.interface-design/system.md` as CSS custom properties. Install Plus Jakarta Sans via Google Fonts. Set up the project file structure.

## Why

All subsequent issues (types, components, pages) depend on the project skeleton, design tokens, and shadcn/ui components being in place.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm run dev` starts without errors
- [ ] Plus Jakarta Sans loads as the default font (imported via `next/font/google` or `<link>` in layout)
- [ ] Design system tokens from `.interface-design/system.md` are available as CSS custom properties in `globals.css` (all color, spacing, depth, radius, and typography tokens)
- [ ] shadcn/ui components render with the custom theme — amber accent (`--vest`), warm cream surfaces (`--briefing`), navy text (`--uniform`)

## Technical Details

### Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Next.js 15 + all dependencies (see Dependencies section) |
| `tsconfig.json` | TypeScript config with `@/` path alias mapping to `src/` |
| `next.config.ts` | Next.js configuration |
| `postcss.config.mjs` | PostCSS with `@tailwindcss/postcss` for Tailwind 4 |
| `src/app/globals.css` | Tailwind directives + ALL design system CSS variables |
| `src/lib/utils.ts` | `cn()` helper using `clsx` + `tailwind-merge` |
| `src/app/layout.tsx` | Minimal root layout (placeholder — will be replaced in Layout Shell issue) |

### Dependencies to Install

```
next@^15 react@^19 react-dom@^19 typescript@^5.7
tailwindcss@^4 @tailwindcss/postcss@^4
lucide-react recharts
class-variance-authority clsx tailwind-merge
```

### shadcn/ui Components to Install

Button, Card, Input, Badge, Tabs, Progress, Avatar, DropdownMenu, Sheet, Tooltip, ScrollArea, Dialog, Separator, RadioGroup, Label

Use `npx shadcn@latest init` then `npx shadcn@latest add <component>`.

### Design System Tokens

Read `.interface-design/system.md` and map ALL tokens to CSS custom properties in `globals.css`:

- **Colors:** `--briefing`, `--briefing-elevated`, `--briefing-top`, `--uniform`, `--uniform-secondary`, `--uniform-tertiary`, `--uniform-muted`, `--vest`, `--vest-hover`, `--vest-light`, `--vest-subtle`, `--cleared`, `--cleared-light`, `--alert`, `--alert-light`, `--caution`, `--caution-light`, `--dispatch`, `--dispatch-light`, `--perimeter`, `--perimeter-soft`, `--perimeter-emphasis`, `--perimeter-focus`, `--control-bg`, `--control-border`, `--control-focus`
- **Spacing:** `--space-1` through `--space-16`
- **Depth:** `--shadow-lifted`, `--shadow-raised`, `--shadow-floating`
- **Radius:** `--radius-sm`, `--radius-md`, `--radius-lg`, `--radius-xl`, `--radius-full`

### Font Setup

Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts with weights 400, 500, 600, 700.

## Scope Boundaries

**In scope:** Project init, dependency install, design tokens, shadcn/ui setup, `cn()` util, minimal layout placeholder.

**Out of scope:** Navigation components (Layout Shell issue), types/mock data (Types issue), page content.

## Design Reference

Read `.interface-design/system.md` for the complete design system. Key decisions:
- Depth: Subtle shadows (Lifted/Raised/Floating levels)
- Palette: Amber `--vest` accent, navy `--uniform` text, cream `--briefing` surfaces
- Typography: Plus Jakarta Sans
- Radius: sm(6) / md(10) / lg(14) / xl(20) / full(9999)

## Resolver Instructions

**Step 0.5 — Read the design system:** Before writing any code, read `.interface-design/system.md` and apply its tokens.

1. Run `npx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --import-alias "@/*"` (or equivalent manual setup for Next.js 15 + Tailwind 4)
2. Install additional dependencies
3. Initialize shadcn/ui and install all listed components
4. Create `globals.css` with all CSS custom properties from system.md
5. Create `src/lib/utils.ts` with `cn()` helper
6. Verify `npm run dev` works and shadcn components render with custom tokens

issues.title: [Foundation] TypeScript Types & Mock Data Layer
issues.description:
## Context

The GuardTrack platform needs a rich, realistic mock data layer. All pages consume this data. The mock data should feel like a real Dutch security training company — Dutch guard names, real Dutch festivals, realistic certification names, and proper Dutch course titles and descriptions.

## What

Define all TypeScript interfaces for the data model and create comprehensive Dutch mock data files covering guards, courses, events, notifications, quiz questions, and practical scenarios.

## Why

Every page (Dashboard, Catalog, Course Detail, Profile) depends on typed mock data. Creating this as a standalone layer ensures consistency across all pages and makes it easy to later swap mock data for real API calls.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All types are well-documented with JSDoc comments explaining each field
- [ ] Mock data is realistic and fully Dutch (names, events, certifications, course titles/descriptions)
- [ ] At least 3 courses are mandatory with deadlines tied to upcoming events (e.g., "Verplicht voor Lowlands Festival")

## Technical Details

### Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/lib/types.ts` | All TypeScript interfaces: `Guard`, `Course`, `CourseModule`, `Lesson`, `QuizQuestion`, `Scenario`, `Event`, `Certification`, `Badge`, `Notification`, `LeaderboardEntry`, `Category`, `Deployment` |
| `src/lib/constants.ts` | Course categories (Crowd Control, EHBO, Communicatie, Toegangscontrole, Brandveiligheid, Noodprocedures), difficulty levels (Beginner, Gemiddeld, Gevorderd), course statuses |
| `src/data/mock/guards.ts` | Current guard profile (Jan de Vries) + 8-10 other guards for leaderboard |
| `src/data/mock/courses.ts` | 12-15 courses with Dutch titles, descriptions, modules with lessons, durations, categories, difficulty, mandatory/optional flag |
| `src/data/mock/events.ts` | 6-8 upcoming and past Dutch events: Koningsdag Amsterdam, Lowlands Festival, Zwarte Cross, Bevrijdingsdag, Mysteryland, SAIL Amsterdam, Vierdaagsefeesten |
| `src/data/mock/notifications.ts` | 8-10 notifications: overdue courses, new assignments, expiring certs, event announcements |
| `src/data/mock/quiz-questions.ts` | 3-4 questions per course for 4-5 courses (multiple choice, true/false, scenario-based) |
| `src/data/mock/scenarios.ts` | 4-5 practical scenario exercises: crowd surge response, medical emergency at festival, aggressive visitor de-escalation, evacuation procedure, lost child protocol |

### Type Design Guidelines

- Use string literal unions for enums (e.g., `difficulty: 'beginner' | 'gemiddeld' | 'gevorderd'`)
- Use `Date` or ISO string for dates
- Each `Course` should contain `modules: CourseModule[]`, each module contains `lessons: Lesson[]`
- Each `Lesson` has a `type: 'video' | 'reading' | 'quiz' | 'scenario'`
- `QuizQuestion` supports `type: 'multiple-choice' | 'true-false'` with `correctAnswer`, `explanation`, and `options`

### Mock Data Requirements

- **Guards:** Dutch names (Jan de Vries, Fatima el-Amrani, Pieter Bakker, etc.), roles, company
- **Courses:** Dutch titles like "Crowd Control bij Grote Evenementen", "EHBO op Festivals", "Communicatie met Bezoekers", "Toegangscontrole & Fouilleren"
- **Events:** Real Dutch events with realistic dates in 2026
- **Scenarios:** Contextual situations at specific festivals ("Je bent geplaatst bij de hoofdingang van Lowlands Festival...")
- **Quiz questions:** In Dutch with explanations

## Scope Boundaries

**In scope:** Type definitions, constants, all mock data files.

**Out of scope:** Components, pages, API integration, data fetching hooks.

**Files this issue does NOT touch:** Any files in `src/components/`, `src/app/` (except indirectly via imports).

## Resolver Instructions

1. Read the technical spec Section 2 (Architecture) for the full type list
2. Create `src/lib/types.ts` first — all interfaces with JSDoc
3. Create `src/lib/constants.ts` — categories, difficulty levels, statuses
4. Create mock data files one by one, importing types from `types.ts`
5. Ensure all mock data is internally consistent (courses reference valid categories, events reference valid courses, etc.)
6. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify type correctness

issues.title: [Foundation] Layout Shell & Navigation
issues.description:
## Context

The GuardTrack platform needs a responsive app shell that adapts between desktop (sidebar) and mobile (bottom tabs). All 4 pages render within this layout. The navigation uses the amber `--vest` accent for active states.

## What

Build the responsive app shell: desktop sidebar (collapsible, same background as canvas with border separation), mobile bottom tab bar (4 tabs), and a sticky top bar with search, notification bell, and user avatar.

## Why

All pages render within this layout shell. Navigation and the top bar are shared across every page. This must be in place before any page can be built.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sidebar visible at >=768px, bottom tabs visible at <768px (responsive switch)
- [ ] Active page highlighted with amber `--vest` accent on icon + label
- [ ] Sidebar collapses to icon-only mode with a toggle button, with smooth 250ms transition

## Technical Details

### Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/app/layout.tsx` | Root layout — switches between sidebar (>=768px) and bottom tabs (<768px), includes top bar, wraps children in main content area with proper padding |
| `src/components/layout/app-sidebar.tsx` | Desktop sidebar: logo at top, 4 nav items with Lucide icons (LayoutDashboard, BookOpen, GraduationCap, User), active amber accent, collapsible to icon-only, same background as canvas with right border |
| `src/components/layout/bottom-tabs.tsx` | Mobile: fixed bottom bar, 4 tabs matching sidebar items, active amber accent, notification badge on relevant tab |
| `src/components/layout/top-bar.tsx` | Sticky top: page title (dynamic based on route), search input, notification bell with unread count badge, user avatar dropdown |
| `src/components/shared/notification-item.tsx` | Single notification row: icon, message, time ago, read/unread state |

### Navigation Items

| Label | Icon | Route |
|---|---|---|
| Dashboard | `LayoutDashboard` | `/` |
| Catalogus | `BookOpen` | `/catalogus` |
| Cursussen | `GraduationCap` | `/cursus` |
| Profiel | `User` | `/profiel` |

### Design System Application

- **Sidebar background:** `--briefing` (same as canvas), right border `--perimeter`
- **Active nav item:** `--vest` color on icon + label text (NOT background fill)
- **Top bar:** Sticky, `--briefing-elevated` background, bottom border `--perimeter-soft`
- **Search input:** `--control-bg` background, `--control-border`, focus ring `--perimeter-focus`
- **Notification badge:** `--alert` background, white text, `--radius-full`
- **Avatar:** `--vest` background with white initials, `--radius-full`
- **Collapse transition:** 250ms ease-in-out (per system.md animation spec)

### Responsive Behavior

- Desktop (>=768px): Sidebar + top bar + main content
- Mobile (<768px): Top bar + main content + bottom tabs (fixed)
- Main content area should have appropriate padding and scroll independently

## Scope Boundaries

**In scope:** Root layout, sidebar, bottom tabs, top bar, notification item component.

**Out of scope:** Page content (placeholder children only), notification dropdown panel (just the bell icon + badge), search functionality (just the input UI).

**Files this issue does NOT touch:** `src/data/mock/*`, `src/components/shared/` (except `notification-item.tsx`), page files.

## Existing Code

- `src/lib/utils.ts` — `cn()` helper (from scaffolding issue)
- `src/app/globals.css` — Design system CSS variables (from scaffolding issue)
- shadcn/ui components: Sheet (for mobile sidebar), Avatar, Badge, Input, DropdownMenu, Tooltip, ScrollArea

## Resolver Instructions

**Step 0.5 — Read the design system:** Before writing any code, read `.interface-design/system.md` and apply its tokens.

1. Read `.interface-design/system.md` for navigation patterns (Section: Navigation)
2. Build `app-sidebar.tsx` first — desktop sidebar with collapsible behavior
3. Build `bottom-tabs.tsx` — mobile tab bar
4. Build `top-bar.tsx` — sticky header with search + notifications + avatar
5. Build `notification-item.tsx` — single notification row component
6. Wire everything together in `layout.tsx` with responsive breakpoint detection
7. Test navigation links work (pages can be placeholder `<div>` for now)
8. Verify responsive switch works at 768px breakpoint

issues.title: [Components] Shared UI Components
issues.description:
## Context

The GuardTrack platform has a set of shared components used across multiple pages. The signature element is the shield-shaped progress indicator — it appears on course cards, the dashboard, achievement badges, and the profile page. All components must use design system tokens exclusively.

## What

Build 9 shared UI components: shield progress indicator (signature element), deployment readiness bar, course card, category card, badge/tag, stats card, leaderboard row, certification card, and deployment card.

## Why

All 4 pages (Dashboard, Catalog, Course Detail, Profile) depend on these shared components. Building them as a standalone component library ensures visual consistency and reusability.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shield progress animates fill on mount using a CSS transition (not just a static fill)
- [ ] All components are responsive — render correctly at 375px and 1280px widths
- [ ] Components use design system tokens exclusively — no hardcoded colors, spacing, or font sizes (verify by searching for hex values, rgb(), raw px values)

## Technical Details

### Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/components/shared/shield-progress.tsx` | SVG shield shape with animated fill level (0-100%), optional icon/number overlay, sizes (sm/md/lg), color variants (amber default, green complete, red overdue) |
| `src/components/shared/deployment-readiness.tsx` | Horizontal bar: event name + date on left, required course shields in a row, overall status badge (Gereed/Bezig/Verlopen), warm amber gradient when complete |
| `src/components/shared/course-card.tsx` | Rich card: gradient thumbnail by category, title, duration + difficulty badge, shield progress (bottom-right), mandatory/optional tag, category icon, enrollment count. Hover lifts shadow (150ms). |
| `src/components/shared/category-card.tsx` | Category card: large Lucide icon, category name, course count, warm accent on hover. Used in catalog grid. |
| `src/components/shared/badge-tag.tsx` | Versatile badge: variants for mandatory (amber), optional (gray), difficulty levels (green/amber/red), status (active/completed/overdue), cert validity. Uses Micro typography, uppercase. |
| `src/components/shared/stats-card.tsx` | Stat display: large number (Display typography — 2rem/700), label (Caption — 13px/500), optional trend indicator (up/down arrow with percentage), optional icon. |
| `src/components/shared/leaderboard-row.tsx` | Row: rank (1-3 get medal icons), avatar, guard name, points, badges count, streak (flame icon). Current user row highlighted with `--vest-subtle`. |
| `src/components/shared/cert-card.tsx` | Cert card: name, issuing body, earned date, expiry with countdown, status badge (Geldig/Verloopt Binnenkort/Verlopen), shield icon. Expiring soon = `--caution`, expired = `--alert`. |
| `src/components/shared/deployment-card.tsx` | Past deployment: event name, date range, location, role, linked required courses, status badge. |

### Shield Progress Component (Signature Element)

This is the most important component — it defines the product's visual identity.

- SVG-based shield shape (classic badge/patch silhouette)
- Fill level animates from 0 to target percentage on mount
- Three sizes: `sm` (24px), `md` (40px), `lg` (64px)
- Color variants: default `--vest` amber, complete `--cleared` green, overdue `--alert` red
- Optional overlay: icon (checkmark for complete) or number (percentage)
- Use CSS `clip-path` or SVG `clipPath` for the shield shape with a rect fill that transitions height

### Component Props Pattern

All components should accept:
- Their specific data props (typed from `src/lib/types.ts`)
- `className?: string` for composition
- Use `cn()` from `src/lib/utils.ts` for class merging

### Design System References

- Cards: `--briefing-elevated` surface, `--perimeter` border, `--radius-lg`, `--space-5` padding, Lifted shadow
- Hover: shadow transitions to Raised over 150ms
- Badges: semantic light background + full color text, `--radius-sm`, Micro typography uppercase
- Typography scale: Display (32px/700), Heading (20px/600), Body (15px/400), Caption (13px/500), Micro (11px/600)

## Scope Boundaries

**In scope:** All 9 shared components listed above.

**Out of scope:** Page layouts, search/filter bar (Catalog issue), quiz/scenario components (Course Detail issue), skill radar (Profile issue), layout components (Layout Shell issue).

**Files this issue does NOT touch:** `src/app/*` (pages), `src/components/layout/*`, `src/data/mock/*`.

## Existing Code

- `src/lib/types.ts` — TypeScript interfaces (from Types issue)
- `src/lib/constants.ts` — Categories, difficulty levels (from Types issue)
- `src/lib/utils.ts` — `cn()` helper
- `src/app/globals.css` — All design system CSS variables
- shadcn/ui: Card, Badge, Avatar, Progress, Tooltip

## Resolver Instructions

**Step 0.5 — Read the design system:** Before writing any code, read `.interface-design/system.md` and apply its tokens.

1. Read `.interface-design/system.md` for component patterns (Cards, Badges/Tags, Signature Patterns sections)
2. Read `src/lib/types.ts` to understand the data shapes each component receives
3. Start with `shield-progress.tsx` — the signature element. Get the SVG shape and animation right first.
4. Build `badge-tag.tsx` next — used by many other components
5. Build remaining components, each importing types and using `cn()` + design tokens
6. Verify no hardcoded colors exist (search for `#`, `rgb(`, `hsl(` outside of globals.css)

issues.title: [Pages] Dashboard Page
issues.description:
## Context

The Dashboard is the home page — the first thing a guard sees when they open GuardTrack. It should immediately answer: "What do I need to do?" and "Am I ready for my next deployment?" The layout follows a briefing-style approach.

## What

Build the Dashboard page with 6 sections: greeting + deployment readiness, my courses, upcoming deployments, notifications, leaderboard, and statistics.

## Why

This is the primary entry point. It surfaces the most actionable information: upcoming deployment readiness, in-progress courses, and overdue items.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Deployment readiness bar is prominently positioned at the top, clearly showing which mandatory courses are complete/incomplete for the next event
- [ ] Course cards scroll horizontally on mobile (<768px), display as a grid on desktop (>=768px)
- [ ] All content is in Dutch and the layout is fully responsive (stacks vertically on mobile, uses grid on desktop)

## Technical Details

### Files to Create/Modify

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/app/page.tsx` | Full dashboard page with all 6 sections |

### Page Sections (top to bottom)

1. **Greeting + Deployment Readiness**
   - "Goedemorgen, Jan" with current date
   - `DeploymentReadiness` component for the next upcoming event
   - Shows which mandatory courses still need completion

2. **Mijn Cursussen (My Courses)**
   - Horizontal scrollable row of `CourseCard` components
   - Filter tabs: Alles / Verplicht / Optioneel (use shadcn Tabs)
   - Each card shows shield progress, "Ga verder" button
   - Desktop: 3-column grid, Mobile: horizontal scroll with `overflow-x-auto`

3. **Aankomende Inzetten (Upcoming Deployments)**
   - Timeline/card view of next 3-4 events
   - Each event shows: name, date, location, small shield indicators for required courses

4. **Meldingen (Notifications)**
   - Last 5 `NotificationItem` components
   - Color-coded: overdue (`--alert`), new assignments (`--dispatch`), expiring certs (`--caution`)
   - "Bekijk alle meldingen" link at bottom

5. **Klassement (Leaderboard)**
   - Top 5 `LeaderboardRow` components
   - Current user (Jan de Vries) highlighted even if not in top 5
   - Medals for positions 1-3

6. **Statistieken (Stats)**
   - 4 `StatsCard` components in a 2x2 grid (mobile) or 4-column row (desktop):
     - Cursussen Voltooid (courses completed)
     - Gemiddelde Score (average quiz score)
     - Actieve Streak (day streak)
     - Certificaten (valid certs count)

### Layout Pattern

- Desktop: 2-column grid for main content (courses + deployments side by side), full-width for readiness bar and stats
- Mobile: single column, sections stacked
- Section headers use Heading typography (20px/600)
- Sections separated by `--space-8` (32px)

## Scope Boundaries

**In scope:** Dashboard page assembly using existing shared components and mock data.

**Out of scope:** Creating new shared components (use existing ones), modifying the layout shell, notification dropdown panel.

**Files this issue does NOT touch:** `src/components/shared/*` (uses them, doesn't modify), `src/components/layout/*`, `src/data/mock/*`.

## Existing Code

- `src/components/shared/deployment-readiness.tsx` — Deployment readiness bar
- `src/components/shared/course-card.tsx` — Course card with shield progress
- `src/components/shared/notification-item.tsx` — Notification row
- `src/components/shared/leaderboard-row.tsx` — Leaderboard entry
- `src/components/shared/stats-card.tsx` — Statistics display
- `src/data/mock/courses.ts`, `events.ts`, `notifications.ts`, `guards.ts` — Mock data
- `src/lib/types.ts` — TypeScript interfaces
- shadcn/ui: Tabs, Card, ScrollArea

## Resolver Instructions

**Step 0.5 — Read the design system:** Before writing any code, read `.interface-design/system.md` and apply its tokens.

1. Read all mock data files to understand the available data
2. Read existing shared components to understand their props
3. Build the page top-to-bottom, section by section
4. Use `'use client'` directive since the page has interactive filter tabs
5. Import mock data directly (no fetching needed)
6. Verify responsive behavior: horizontal scroll on mobile for courses, grid on desktop
7. Ensure deployment readiness bar is the visual hero of the page

issues.title: [Pages] Course Catalog Page
issues.description:
## Context

The Course Catalog ("Cursuscatalogus") is where guards browse and discover training courses. It needs robust search and filtering since guards often need to find specific mandatory courses for upcoming deployments quickly.

## What

Build the Course Catalog page with search, multi-filter support, category browsing, a recommended section, and a responsive course grid. Also create a reusable search/filter bar component.

## Why

Guards need to quickly find relevant training — especially mandatory courses for their upcoming deployments. The catalog is the primary discovery interface.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Search filters courses by title and description in real-time (client-side, case-insensitive)
- [ ] Filters work in combination — selecting a category AND a difficulty level narrows results correctly
- [ ] When filters produce no results, a friendly empty state is shown: "Geen cursussen gevonden" with a suggestion to broaden filters

## Technical Details

### Files to Create/Modify

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/app/catalogus/page.tsx` | Full catalog page with all sections |
| `src/components/shared/search-filter-bar.tsx` | Combined search input + filter dropdowns (category, difficulty, status, type) |

### Page Sections (top to bottom)

1. **Page Header** — "Cursuscatalogus" (Display typography), subtitle with total course count

2. **Search + Filters** (`SearchFilterBar` component)
   - Search input: prominently sized, placeholder "Zoek een cursus..."
   - Filter dropdowns/pills: Categorie (6 categories), Niveau (Beginner/Gemiddeld/Gevorderd), Type (Alles/Verplicht/Optioneel), Status (Alles/Niet gestart/Bezig/Voltooid)
   - Active filters shown as dismissible chips below the bar
   - "Wis filters" (clear all) button when any filter is active

3. **Aanbevolen voor jou (Recommended)**
   - 3-4 course cards with reason tags: "Verplicht voor Lowlands", "Versterk je EHBO kennis"
   - Horizontal layout, distinct from main grid (slightly elevated background)

4. **Categorieën (Categories)**
   - Grid of 6 `CategoryCard` components: Crowd Control, EHBO, Communicatie, Toegangscontrole, Brandveiligheid, Noodprocedures
   - Clicking a category card sets the category filter (scrolls to/updates the grid below)

5. **Alle Cursussen (All Courses)**
   - Grid of `CourseCard` components: 3 columns desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile
   - Filtered by search query + active filters
   - Each card links to `/cursus/[id]`
   - Result count shown: "X cursussen gevonden"

6. **Empty State**
   - When filters match nothing: illustration/icon, "Geen cursussen gevonden", "Probeer andere zoektermen of filters"

### Filter Logic (client-side)

```typescript
// All filters are AND-combined
const filtered = courses.filter(course => {
  const matchesSearch = !query || 
    course.title.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()) ||
    course.description.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase());
  const matchesCategory = !categoryFilter || course.category === categoryFilter;
  const matchesDifficulty = !difficultyFilter || course.difficulty === difficultyFilter;
  const matchesType = !typeFilter || 
    (typeFilter === 'verplicht' ? course.mandatory : !course.mandatory);
  const matchesStatus = !statusFilter || course.status === statusFilter;
  return matchesSearch && matchesCategory && matchesDifficulty && matchesType && matchesStatus;
});
```

### SearchFilterBar Component Props

```typescript
interface SearchFilterBarProps {
  query: string;
  onQueryChange: (query: string) => void;
  filters: {
    category: string | null;
    difficulty: string | null;
    type: string | null;
    status: string | null;
  };
  onFilterChange: (key: string, value: string | null) => void;
  onClearAll: () => void;
  categories: Category[];
}
```

## Scope Boundaries

**In scope:** Catalog page, search/filter bar component, client-side filtering, category card interaction.

**Out of scope:** Server-side search, URL query params for filters (nice-to-have but not required), pagination.

**Files this issue does NOT touch:** Other pages, layout components, existing shared components (uses but doesn't modify).

## Existing Code

- `src/components/shared/course-card.tsx` — Course card with shield progress
- `src/components/shared/category-card.tsx` — Category display card
- `src/components/shared/badge-tag.tsx` — Status/filter badges
- `src/data/mock/courses.ts` — 12-15 courses with categories, difficulty, mandatory flag
- `src/lib/constants.ts` — Category list, difficulty levels
- `src/lib/types.ts` — Course, Category interfaces
- shadcn/ui: Input, Badge, DropdownMenu, Tabs, Card

## Resolver Instructions

**Step 0.5 — Read the design system:** Before writing any code, read `.interface-design/system.md` and apply its tokens.

1. Read mock data (`courses.ts`, `constants.ts`) to understand available data
2. Build `search-filter-bar.tsx` first — reusable filter component
3. Build `catalogus/page.tsx` with `'use client'` directive for interactive state
4. Implement filter logic using `useState` + `useMemo`
5. Wire category cards to set the category filter
6. Test: search for partial text, combine multiple filters, verify empty state appears
7. Verify responsive grid: 3 cols desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile

issues.title: [Pages] Course Detail & Lesson Page
issues.description:
## Context The Course Detail page is the learning environment — the most complex page in the application. Guards consume different content types (video, reading, quizzes, scenarios) within a module-based course structure. Quizzes support two modes: practice (instant feedback) and test (end-of-quiz scoring). ## What Build the Course Detail page with a module sidebar/accordion, lesson content area, and 4 content-type components: video player placeholder, reading content, quiz question (with practice + test modes), and scenario exercise. ## Why This is the core learning experience. Guards spend most of their time here consuming training content and completing assessments. The dual quiz mode (practice vs. test) is essential for both learning and certification. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Quiz practice mode ("Oefenmodus"): after selecting an answer, immediately shows correct/incorrect feedback with Dutch explanation, allows retrying wrong answers - [ ] Quiz test mode ("Toetsmodus"): no per-question feedback, all answers collected, results shown at end with total score, pass/fail indicator, and per-question review - [ ] Module sidebar shows clear course structure with completion states — each lesson has a shield mini-icon (empty/half/full) and a type icon (video/reading/quiz/scenario) ## Technical Details ### Files to Create/Modify | File | Purpose | |---|---| | `src/app/cursus/[id]/page.tsx` | Course detail page with module navigation and lesson content area | | `src/components/shared/video-player-placeholder.tsx` | Placeholder video player: 16:9 container, play button overlay, chapter markers, "Video niet beschikbaar in demo" message | | `src/components/shared/reading-content.tsx` | Rich text renderer: headings, paragraphs, bullets, info/warning callouts, checklists, expandable sections | | `src/components/shared/quiz-question.tsx` | Quiz with Oefenmodus (practice) and Toetsmodus (test) modes | | `src/components/shared/scenario-exercise.tsx` | Practical scenario: situation description, response options, feedback with expert explanation | ### Page Layout **Course Header:** - Course title, category badge, difficulty badge, mandatory/optional tag - Duration, module count, overall shield progress (lg size) - "Start cursus" / "Ga verder" primary button **Module Sidebar (desktop) / Accordion (mobile):** - Collapsible list of modules, each containing lessons - Per-lesson: shield mini-icon (completion state) + type icon + lesson title - Current lesson highlighted with `--vest-subtle` background - Desktop: fixed sidebar (280px width), Mobile: collapsible accordion above content **Lesson Content Area:** - Renders component based on `lesson.type`: - `'video'` → `VideoPlayerPlaceholder` - `'reading'` → `ReadingContent` - `'quiz'` → `QuizQuestion` with mode toggle tabs - `'scenario'` → `ScenarioExercise` **Lesson Navigation:** - Previous / Next buttons at bottom - Progress bar for current module - Auto-advance to next module when current module complete ### Quiz Component State Machine **Practice Mode (Oefenmodus):** ``` IDLE → ANSWERING (user selects option) → FEEDBACK (show correct/incorrect + explanation) → if correct: NEXT_QUESTION → if incorrect: allow RETRY or NEXT_QUESTION → ALL_DONE → show summary ``` **Test Mode (Toetsmodus):** ``` IDLE → ANSWERING → NEXT_QUESTION (no feedback) → ... → ALL_DONE → RESULTS (show score, pass/fail at 70%, per-question review with correct answers) ``` ### Quiz Component Props ```typescript interface QuizQuestionProps { questions: QuizQuestion[]; mode: 'practice' | 'test'; onComplete?: (score: number, total: number) => void; } ``` ### Scenario Component - Shows situation description with context (e.g., "Je bent geplaatst bij de hoofdingang van Lowlands Festival. Een groep van 15 personen probeert zonder geldige tickets het terrein op te komen...") - Multiple response options (3-4 choices) - After selection: expert feedback explaining best practice - Score indicator: "Hoe deed je het?" with rating ### Video Player Placeholder - 16:9 aspect ratio container with dark background - Large play button icon centered - "Video niet beschikbaar in demo" overlay text - Chapter markers below (clickable but non-functional) - Playback speed selector (visual only) - Captions toggle (visual only) ### Reading Content - Renders rich text with: `

`, `

`, `

`, `

    `, `
      ` - Info callout: `--dispatch-light` background, `--dispatch` left border, info icon - Warning callout: `--caution-light` background, `--caution` left border, alert icon - Checklist: checkable items (client-side state only) - Expandable "Meer informatie" accordion sections ## Scope Boundaries **In scope:** Course detail page, module sidebar/accordion, 4 content-type components, lesson navigation, quiz modes. **Out of scope:** Real video playback, progress persistence (mock only), PDF downloads, course enrollment flow. **Files this issue does NOT touch:** Other pages, layout components, existing shared components (uses course-card, badge-tag, shield-progress but doesn't modify them). ## Existing Code - `src/components/shared/shield-progress.tsx` — Shield indicator (sm size for module sidebar) - `src/components/shared/badge-tag.tsx` — Category, difficulty, mandatory badges - `src/data/mock/courses.ts` — Courses with modules and lessons - `src/data/mock/quiz-questions.ts` — Quiz question banks per course - `src/data/mock/scenarios.ts` — Scenario exercises - `src/lib/types.ts` — Course, CourseModule, Lesson, QuizQuestion, Scenario interfaces - shadcn/ui: Tabs, Card, RadioGroup, Label, Dialog, ScrollArea, Separator ## Resolver Instructions **Step 0.5 — Read the design system:** Before writing any code, read `.interface-design/system.md` and apply its tokens. 1. Read mock data files (`courses.ts`, `quiz-questions.ts`, `scenarios.ts`) thoroughly 2. Build `video-player-placeholder.tsx` first (simplest content component) 3. Build `reading-content.tsx` with all rich text elements 4. Build `quiz-question.tsx` with both modes — this is the most complex component 5. Build `scenario-exercise.tsx` 6. Build `cursus/[id]/page.tsx` — wire module sidebar + content area + navigation 7. Use `'use client'` — heavy interactive state (current lesson, quiz state, sidebar collapse) 8. Test: navigate between lessons, complete a quiz in both modes, verify module completion states update

issues.title: [Pages] User Profile Page
issues.description:
## Context

The Profile page is the guard's personal record — certifications, course history, deployment history, and a skills radar chart. It should feel accomplishment-focused and encouraging, showcasing what the guard has achieved.

## What

Build the User Profile page with 5 sections: profile header with stats, skills radar chart, certifications & badges, course history, and deployment history. Also create the radar chart component using Recharts.

## Why

Guards need to see their certifications (especially expiring ones), track their learning progress across skill categories, and review their deployment history. This page motivates continued training.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Radar chart renders correctly with 6 axes (Crowd Control, EHBO, Communicatie, Toegangscontrole, Brandveiligheid, Noodprocedures) and is responsive at all breakpoints
- [ ] Certifications are sorted by expiry date (soonest first), with visual amber warning for expiring soon (`--caution`) and red alert for expired (`--alert`)
- [ ] All content is in Dutch and the layout is fully responsive (sections stack on mobile, grid on desktop)

## Technical Details

### Files to Create/Modify

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/app/profiel/page.tsx` | Full profile page with all 5 sections |
| `src/components/shared/skill-radar.tsx` | Radar/spider chart using Recharts RadarChart — amber fill, navy outline, 6 skill axes |

### Page Sections (top to bottom)

1. **Profile Header**
   - Large avatar: initials-based, `--vest` background, `--radius-full` (use shadcn Avatar)
   - Full name: "Jan de Vries" (Display typography)
   - Role: "Beveiligingsmedewerker" (Body typography, `--uniform-secondary`)
   - Company + member since date
   - 3 inline stats: Cursussen Voltooid | Gemiddelde Score | Totaal Punten (Display numbers)

2. **Vaardigheden (Skills Radar)**
   - `SkillRadar` component using Recharts `RadarChart`
   - 6 axes: Crowd Control, EHBO, Communicatie, Toegangscontrole, Brandveiligheid, Noodprocedures
   - Values 0-100, calculated from completed courses per category in mock data
   - Amber (`--vest`) fill with 30% opacity, navy (`--uniform`) stroke
   - Wrapped in `ResponsiveContainer` for responsiveness
   - Legend below the chart showing category names

3. **Certificaten & Badges**
   - **Certificaten:** Grid of `CertCard` components. Sort by expiry. Expiring within 30 days = `--caution` warning. Expired = `--alert`.
   - **Badges:** Grid of shield-shaped achievement badges:
     - "Eerste Cursus" (first course completed)
     - "5 Cursussen Voltooid"
     - "Perfect Score" (100% on a quiz)
     - "30 Dagen Streak"
     - "EHBO Expert" (all EHBO courses)
     - "Nachtdienst Veteraan" (deployment badge)
   - Each badge: shield icon, name, date earned. Use `ShieldProgress` component at lg size with checkmark overlay.

4. **Cursusgeschiedenis (Course History)**
   - Table/list of completed and in-progress courses
   - Columns: course name, category, completion date, quiz score average, status badge
   - Filter tabs: Alles / Voltooid / Bezig (use shadcn Tabs)
   - Sortable by date or score (client-side)

5. **Inzetgeschiedenis (Deployment History)**
   - List of `DeploymentCard` components (5-6 past deployments)
   - Most recent first
   - Each shows: event name, date range, location, role, linked training courses

### Skill Radar Component

```typescript
interface SkillRadarProps {
  skills: { category: string; level: number }[];
  className?: string;
}
```

Uses Recharts:
- `RadarChart` with `PolarGrid`, `PolarAngleAxis`, `PolarRadiusAxis`, `Radar`
- Amber fill: `var(--vest)` with `fillOpacity={0.3}`
- Navy stroke: `var(--uniform)`
- Grid lines: `var(--perimeter)`
- Tick labels: Caption typography

### Badge Data (hardcoded in page)

```typescript
const badges = [
  { name: 'Eerste Cursus', icon: 'BookOpen', earned: '2024-03-15' },
  { name: '5 Cursussen Voltooid', icon: 'Award', earned: '2024-06-01' },
  { name: 'Perfect Score', icon: 'Star', earned: '2024-08-20' },
  { name: '30 Dagen Streak', icon: 'Flame', earned: '2024-10-01' },
  { name: 'EHBO Expert', icon: 'Heart', earned: '2025-01-15' },
  { name: 'Nachtdienst Veteraan', icon: 'Moon', earned: '2025-04-01' },
];
```

## Scope Boundaries

**In scope:** Profile page, skill radar component, badge display, course history with filters, deployment history.

**Out of scope:** Profile editing, avatar upload, settings, password change, notification preferences.

**Files this issue does NOT touch:** Other pages, layout components, mock data files (uses but doesn't modify).

## Existing Code

- `src/components/shared/cert-card.tsx` — Certification card with expiry status
- `src/components/shared/deployment-card.tsx` — Deployment history entry
- `src/components/shared/shield-progress.tsx` — Shield indicator (used for badges)
- `src/components/shared/stats-card.tsx` — Stat display (used in header)
- `src/data/mock/guards.ts` — Current guard profile (Jan de Vries)
- `src/data/mock/courses.ts` — Course data for history + skill calculation
- `src/data/mock/events.ts` — Past events for deployment history
- `src/lib/types.ts` — Guard, Certification, Deployment, Course interfaces
- shadcn/ui: Avatar, Tabs, Card, Badge, Separator
- `recharts` — RadarChart, PolarGrid, PolarAngleAxis, PolarRadiusAxis, Radar, ResponsiveContainer

## Resolver Instructions

**Step 0.5 — Read the design system:** Before writing any code, read `.interface-design/system.md` and apply its tokens.

1. Read mock data files (`guards.ts`, `courses.ts`, `events.ts`) to understand available data
2. Build `skill-radar.tsx` first — Recharts radar chart with responsive container
3. Build `profiel/page.tsx` top-to-bottom, section by section
4. Use `'use client'` for interactive course history filter tabs
5. Calculate skill levels from mock course completion data per category
6. Sort certifications by expiry, apply visual warnings
7. Test radar chart at all breakpoints (375px, 768px, 1280px)
8. Verify badge shields render at correct size with appropriate icons
